name: Buzzy Drootin
abstract: Benjamin "Buzzy" Drootin (April 22, 1920 – May 21, 2000) was an American jazz drummer.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Dody Goodman in "Dody in Dixieland"
Newport Jazz Festival: Eddie Condon, Lee Wiley, The World's Greatest Jazz Band, and Others
Newport Jazz Festival: Eddie Condon, Lee Wiley, The World's Greatest Jazz Band, and Others

about_quotes.speaker: Wild Bill Davison
about_quotes.evidence: I had a fantastic band and it was against - there were two bands - there were the Dukes of Dixie Land and ourselves and it was a hell of a job... Buzzy Drootin

about_quotes.speaker: Johnny Varro
about_quotes.evidence: Actually it was a duo with Buzzy Drootin on drums and myself. ... I was there for about eight months playing intermission with Buzzy Drootin
about_quotes.doc_title: Johnny Varro interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: There was always good stuff there. There was the Savoy. Next to the Savoy, there was another place which was a basically black club, where they had Sabby Lewis’s band there. ... was Sidney Bechet. They had a terrific band down there, with Doc Cheatham on trumpet, a very good local drummer, Buzzy Dro
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Sherman
about_quotes.evidence: But then my favorite drummers were George Wettling with Condon, Buzzy Drootin and the great Cliff Leeman. Those were my idols.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Sherman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, June 1, 2006
